steps.title: Find the two unit factor
steps.content:
Use the equivalent form $f(x)=36(81)^x$. When the input increases by $2$, the exponent increases by $2$, so the function value is multiplied by
$$
81^2=6{,}561
$$
Thus $c=6{,}561$.

steps.title: Use the displayed base
steps.content: The question asks which form displays the value of $c$ as the base or coefficient. The form with base $6{,}561$ makes that two-unit growth factor visible: $$ f(x)=36(6{,}561)^{\frac{x}{2}} $$ The answer is D.
